title: Federal Law No. 10: ON PSYCHIATRIC HEALTH
text:
Federal
Law No. 10

Issued
on 27/11/2023

Corresponding
to 13/05/1445 H.

ON
PSYCHIATRIC
HEALTH 




No
amendments were added to this Federal Law concerning the abrogation of Federal
Law No. 28 dated 7/11/1981 concerning the Detention and Treatment of People with
a Mental Disorder based on Article (61) thereof which stipulated that it shall
come into effect after (6) months from its publication (it was published in the
Official Gazette, Issue No. 764 (Annex) dated 30/11/2023). 



We,
Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, President of the United Arab Emirates

-
Pursuant to the perusal of the Constitution;

-
Federal Law No. (1) of 1972 on the Competencies of Ministries and the Powers of
Ministers, and

its
amendments;

-
Federal Law No. (28) of 1981 concerning the Detention and Treatment of People
with a Mental

Disorder;

-
Federal Law No. (5) of 1984 on the Practice of Some Medical Professions by Other
than

Physicians
and Pharmacists;

-
Federal Law No. (5) of 1985 on the Promulgation of the Civil Transactions Law,
and its

amendments;

-
Federal Law No. (43) of 1992 on the Regulation of the Penal Institutions;

-
Federal Law No. (28) of 2005 on the Personal Status, and its amendments;

-
Federal Law No. (29) of 2006 on the Rights of People with Special Needs, and its
amendments;

-
Federal Law No. (2) of 2008 concerning the Civil Associations and Institutions
of Public

Welfare,
and its amendments;

-
Federal Law No. (14) of 2014 on the Control of Communicable Diseases;

-
Federal Law No. (4) of 2015 on Private Health Facilities;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (4) of 2016 on Medical Liability;

-
Federal Law No. (2) of 2019 on the Use of Information and Communications
Technology in the

Health
Fields;

-
Federal Law No. (5) of 2019 on Medical Products and the Profession of Pharmacy
and

Pharmaceutical
Facilities; 

-
Federal Law No. (13) of 2020 on Public Health;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (30) of 2021 on Fighting Narcotics and Psychotropic
Substances, and

its
amendments;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (31) of 2021 on the Promulgation of the Crimes and
Penalties Law, and

its
amendments;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (33) of 2021 on the Regulation of Labour Relations, and
its

amendments;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (38) of 2022 Promulgating the Criminal Procedure
Law;

-
And Federal Decree-Law No. (42) of 2022 Promulgating the Civil Procedure Law;
and

-
Based on the proposal of the Minister of Health and Prevention, the approval by
the Council of

Ministers
and the Federal National Council and the ratification by the Federal Supreme
Council;

Have
issued the following Law:

Chapter
I

Definitions
and General Provisions

Article
1 - Definitions

In
application of the provisions of this Law, the following terms and expressions
shall have the meanings assigned thereto, unless the context requires
otherwise:

UAE:
The United Arab Emirates.

Ministry:
The Ministry of Health and Prevention.

Minister:
The Minister of Health and Prevention.

Health
Entity: The federal or local government entity concerned with health affairs in
the State.

Psychiatric
Health: A state of psychological and social stability enabling the individual
to achieve his goals according to his personal capabilities and to deal with
life pressures, work, produce, and contribute to the community. 

Concerned
Entities: Any federal or local government entity concerned with the protection
of Psychiatric Health in the State or being directly or indirectly related to
the implementation of the provisions of this Law. 

Psychiatric
Health Facility: A health facility licensed to provide Psychiatric Health
services, whether independent or reporting to other health institutions. 

Psychiatric
Health Services: Preventive, therapeutic and rehabilitative services for
psychiatric health.

Committee:
The Control and Follow-up Committee. 

Psychiatric
Disorders: Disturbances in thinking, mood, behaviour, perception, memory, or
other mental abilities, some or all of them, and leading to a defect in the
social, employment, or educational functions or psychological suffering of the
person. These disorders are classified according to the psychiatric
classifications recognized by the competent international organisations and
entities.

Psychiatric
Patient: A person diagnosed with a Psychiatric Disorder. 

Physician:
The physician licensed by the Health Entity to practice the profession. 

Psychiatrist:
The physician licensed by the Health Entity to practice Psychiatry. 

Attending
Physician: The physician who is responsible for examining and following up on
the treatment of the Psychiatric Patient at the Psychiatric Health Facility.


Psychologist:
A person who practices work in a Psychiatric Health Facility, holds a university
degree in Psychology or its equivalent and is licensed by the Health Entity to
practice the profession in accordance with the applicable procedures.

Social
Worker: A person who practices work in a Psychiatric Health Facility, holds a
university degree in Social Work or Sociology or their equivalent and is
licensed by the Health Entity to practice the profession in accordance with the
applicable procedures.

Psychiatric
Nurse: A person who practices work in a Psychiatric Health Facility, holds a
university degree no less than a Diploma in Nursing and is licensed by the
Health Entity to practice the profession in accordance with the applicable
procedures.

Psychological
Counsel: A person who practices work in a Psychiatric Health Facility, holds a
university degree in psychological counselling or its equivalent and is licensed
by the Health Entity to practice the profession in accordance with the
applicable procedures.

Occupational
Therapist: A person who practices work in a Psychiatric Health Facility, holds
a university degree in occupational therapy or its equivalent and is licensed by
the Health Entity to practice the profession in accordance with the applicable
procedures.

Legal
Guardian: The person in charge of the Psychiatric Patient in accordance with
the legislation in force. 

Relatives:
The husband, wife or relatives up to the fourth degree. 

Others:
The employer or consul of the State to which the person subject to the
assessment or treatment belongs or their Representatives. 

Psychiatric
Patient's Representative: The Legal Guardian, Relatives or Others. 

Restraining
Psychiatric Patient : The use of safe methods to restrict the movement of the
Psychiatric Patient. 

Isolation:
Keeping the Psychiatric Patient alone in a safe, closed place allocated for this
purpose for specific periods of time based on the requirements of his treatment,
under the direct supervision of those providing the treatment. 

Placement:
Admitting the person to the Psychiatric Health Facility based on an order issued
by the Competent Judicial Authority. 

Compulsory
Outpatient Therapeutic Care: Subjecting the Psychiatric Patient to treatment
against his will outside the Psychiatric Health Facility. 

Voluntary
Admission: Admitting the Psychiatric Patient to a Psychiatric Health Facility
of his own free will or his representative’s will for the purpose of
treatment.

Emergency
Admission: Admitting the Psychiatric Patient to a Psychiatric Health Facility
in emergency cases for an urgent medical intervention. 

Compulsory
Admission: Admitting the Psychiatric Patient to a Psychiatric Health Facility
against his will in the cases provided for under this Law. 

Urgent
Psychiatric Condition: An acute clinical condition affecting the Psychiatric
Patient, threatening his health or safety or the safety of others and requiring
urgent medical intervention. 

Assessment:
The examination of the person for the purpose of issuing a report on his
psychiatric condition. 

Compulsory
Treatment: Subjecting the Psychiatric Patient to treatment against his will.


Consent
to Treatment: The consent of the Psychiatric Patient or his representative to
receiving treatment after being informed of the therapeutic services to be
provided. 

Bill
of Patient’s Rights: A document showing the rights of the Psychiatric
Patient provided for under this Law, the mechanism for submitting grievances and
complaints and the entity responsible for examining such grievances and
complaints and deciding thereon. 

Article
2 - Objectives of the Law 

This
Law aims to achieve the following objectives:

1-
Organising the relationship between the Psychiatric Patient and the different
parties dealing with him. 

2-
Providing the necessary medical care to the Psychiatric Patient in accordance
with the best standards in force in this field. 

3-
Protecting and preserving the rights and dignity of the Psychiatric Patient.


4-
Reducing the negative effects of Psychiatric Disorders on the life of the
individuals, their families and the community. 

5-
Promoting the integration of the Psychiatric Patient in the community. 

Article
3- Scope of Application

The
provisions of this Law shall apply to any matter related to the Psychiatric
Health, the Psychiatric Patient and the Psychiatric Health Facility and any
other facility in the State concerned with providing care and dealing with the
Psychiatric Patient, as well as to the workers of such facilities, including
those in the free zones. 

Article
4 - Psychiatric Health Services Licensing 

No
Psychiatric Health services may be provided without obtaining a licence from the
Health Entity in accordance with the conditions and controls set forth under the
Implementing Regulation of this Law.

Article
5 - Psychiatric Patients Register 

The
Psychiatric Health Facility shall keep a special register for Psychiatric
Patients. The Implementing Regulation of this Law shall define the data to be
included in this register and the period during which such register shall be
kept. 

Article
6 – The Control and Follow-up Committee

One
committee or more shall be formed in each Emirate by virtue of a decision issued
by the head of the Health Entity. Such committee shall be named the
“Control and Follow-up Committee” and shall provide the Psychiatric
Health services. The aforementioned decision shall define the rules of
procedures of such committees. 

Article
7 - Competencies of the Committee

Without
prejudice to the competencies of the Health Entity, the Committee shall assume
the following competencies:

1-
Follow up on the reports received from the Psychiatric Health Facilities
regarding the Compulsory Admission cases. 

2-
Issue the necessary approvals for the treatment of Psychiatric Patients
rejecting treatment in cases of Compulsory Treatment, based on the request of
the Health Facility. 

3-
Ensure compliance of all Compulsory Admission cases with the conditions provided
for under this Law. 

4-
Oversee the Psychiatric Health Facilities and ensure the commitment of such
facilities and their workers to implement the standards and procedures provided
for under this Law, its Implementing Regulation and the decisions issued
thereunder. 

5-
Review the reports received from the Patients Rights Committee concerning the
complaints and submit recommendations on the same to the Health Entity, if
needed. 

6-
Decide on grievances and objections to the decisions of the Patients' Rights
Committee. 

7-
Decide on the complaints concerning the outpatient psychological treatment
services, centres and clinics. 

8-
Submit reports to the Health Entity on the follow-up and control results. 

c-
Carry out any other competencies determined by a decision of the Minister after
coordination with the Health Entities.

Article
8 - The Decisions of the Committee

1-
The Committee shall issue its decision in the cases provided for under this Law
and should notify the Psychiatric Health Facility of the same within a period of
not more than (6) six working days as of the date on which it was informed of
the case. 

2-
In the event the Committee does not issue its decision within the period defined
hereunder, the director of the Psychiatric Health Facility shall then take the
necessary decisions concerning the Psychiatric Patient in accordance with the
provisions of this Law, provided that the Committee is notified of the same.


Chapter
II

Rights
of the Psychiatric Patient 

Article
9 - General Rights

The
Psychiatric Patient shall have the following rights: 

1-
To receive a comprehensive and understandable explanation of all his rights
directly after being admitted to the Psychiatric Health Facility, including the
right to file a grievance whether personally or through his Representative in
case the Psychiatric Patient was unable to understand. 

2-
To be respected and to be provided with the necessary services in an appropriate
environment that preserves its dignity and meets his needs according to his
health condition. 

3-
To know the nature of his admission to the Health Facility in the event his
condition allows so or to have his Representative informed of the same as
applicable and as soon as possible. 

4-
To preserve his legally established civil rights and not to limit such rights,
except for the purpose of protecting him or Others from harm or according to a
judicial ruling. 

5-
Not to impose restrictions on his work or employment due to his Psychiatric
Disorder or to terminate his service unless by virtue of a report issued by the
Competent Medical Committee and in accordance with the legislation in force in
the State. 

6-
To preserve his privacy and personal belongings at the Psychiatric Health
Facility to which he was admitted. 

7-
To benefit from the communication services unless this has a negative impact on
his psychiatric condition or on Others. 

8-
To receive or refuse to receive visitors according to the visits system adopted
at the Psychiatric Health Facility which shall have the right to limit or to
prevent visits based on the treatment requirements. 

9-
To keep his information confidential in accordance with the legislation in force
in this regard. 

10-
To be protected from degrading treatment, from material, physical and sexual
abuse and others. 

11-
To request the termination of the Compulsory Admission and submit such request
to the Patients’ Rights Committee. 

12-
To file a complaint against any person, entity or Psychiatric Health Facility
without affecting the level of the care provided thereto. 

13-
To seek the assistance of whomever he deems appropriate to represent him before
others and manage his affairs inside or outside the Psychiatric Health
Facility.

14-
To be informed personally or to inform his representative or companion of the
nature of his admission to the Psychiatric Health Facility upon issuance or
renewal of Compulsory Admission decision using an understandable language and
manner and to be informed in writing of all his rights, including the reason for
his admission and the procedures to be taken in case he wishes to leave. 

15-
To leave the Psychiatric Health Facility after the end of the Compulsory
Admission period and obtain the psychological and social care plan. 

16-
To obtain the health insurance which ensures the comprehensive health care in
accordance with the legislation in force in the State. 

17-
To be provided with the necessary support to facilitate the educational process
and the practice of entertainment and cultural activities in coordination with
the Concerned Entities. 

18-
Any other rights determined by a decision of the Minister after coordination
with the Health Entities. 

Article
10 - Rights related to the Psychiatric Patient's Health Care 

When
receiving Psychiatric Health care, the Psychiatric Patient shall have the
following rights: 

1-
To obtain psychiatric treatment and medicines according to the recognised
medical principles. 

2-
To know the diagnosis presented to him; to obtain full information concerning
the treatment plan and its progress, the extent of response to it, any changes
made thereto, the therapeutic methods and the expected benefits, the potential
risks and side effects and the possible therapeutic alternatives before giving
consent to treatment; to be informed of the reasons of his transfer whether
inside or outside the Psychiatric Health Facility as applicable. However, in the
event his health condition does not allow so, the consent shall be obtained from
his legal representative, taking into account the procedures in force in
emergency cases. 

3-
To actively and constantly participate in the therapeutic plan to the extent
that his condition allows so, in order to express his will. 

4-
To obtain the appropriate physical health care.

5-
To be informed or to inform his representative of the name and function of each
member of the therapeutic team providing care at the Psychiatric Health
Facility. 

6-
To receive the due care in a safe and clean environment in accordance with the
standards in force in the Psychiatric Health field. 

7-
Not to undergo any experimental treatment or medical research unless with the
consent of his Representative and after fulfilment of the conditions and
controls prescribed in the legislation in force in the State. 

8-
Not to undergo any treatment without his consent or the consent of his
Representative, except in the cases which are legally provided for. 

9-
To know the health services available at the Psychiatric Health Facility and how
to obtain them, as well as their costs and the coverage of such costs. 

10-
To obtain a comprehensive medical report on his Psychiatric Health condition,
tests and treatment procedures he received during his presence at the
Psychiatric Health Facility. 

11-
To obtain a copy of his medical file in accordance with the provisions of the
Implementing Regulation of this Law.

12-
Any other rights determined by a decision of the Minister after coordination
with the Health Entities.

Article
11 - Minor Psychiatric Patient 

The
minor Psychiatric Patient shall be granted special health guarantees that take
into account his age, psychological condition and best interest, including:


1-
The right to education. 

2-
Requiring the representative of the minor Psychiatric Patient to follow the
treatment plan. 

3-
Any procedure shall be preceded by a preparatory stage by the Social Worker or
Psychologist. 

4-
Special places shall be allocated for the Compulsory Admission of minor
Psychiatric Patients which shall be separated from the adults’ places.
Separated utilities shall be also provided fro the minor Psychiatric Patients.


The
Implementing Regulation of this Law shall define the guarantees and controls of
the Compulsory Admission of a minor Psychiatric Patient in addition to all other
matters related to his rights and the provision of advice and guidance to his
family. 

Article
12 - Bill of Patients’ Rights

The
Psychiatric Health Facility shall undertake to place the Bill of the
Patients’ Rights in a visible place inside the Facility, deliver a copy
thereof to the Psychiatric Patient or his Representative upon admission of the
Psychiatric Patient and attach a copy to the medical file and medical registers,
after the Psychiatric Patient or his Representative signs as to acknowledge
receipt thereof. Such Bill shall be issued in Arabic and any other language
defined by the Health Entity. In all cases, the Psychiatric Health Facility
shall undertake to explain the content of the Bill of the Patients’ Rights
to the Psychiatric Patient or his Representative in a manner that is commensurte
with his capacities. 

Article
13 - The Patients’ Rights Committee 

A
committee shall be formed inside each Psychiatric Health Facility which includes
departments for accommodation of Psychiatric Patients, by virtue of a decision
issued by the head of such facility. This committee shall be named the
“Patients’ Rights Committee”. It shall be presided over by a
Psychiatrist and shall include the following members: 

1-
The Psychologist working in the Psychiatric Health Facility.

2-
The Social Worker working in the Psychiatric Health Facility. 

3-
The Psychiatric Nurse working in the Psychiatric Health Facility. 

The
Director of the Psychiatric Health Facility shall add any members to this
Committee, to be selected from among specialists. 

The
rules of procedures of such committee shall be defined by a decision issued by
the Head of the Psychiatric Health Facility. 

Article
14 - Competencies of the Patients’ Rights Committee

The
Patients’ Rights Committee shall have the following competencies: 

1-
Ensure the respect of the Psychiatric Patients’ rights provided for under
this Law. 

2-
Receive the complaints submitted by the Psychiatric Patients or their
representatives, take all necessary measures with regard thereto and decide on
such complaints. 

3-
Submit a periodic report to the Committee on the complaints. 

Article
15 - Grievance and Objection 

1-
The Psychiatric Patient or his representative may file a grievance against the
decision of the Patients’ Rights Committee before the Committee in
accordance with the controls and procedures set forth under its rules of
procedures. 

2-
The director of the Psychiatric Health Facility or his delegate may object to
the decision of the Patients’ Rights Committee at the Mental Health
Facility before the Committee, in accordance with the procedures and controls
defined under its rules of procedures. 

3-
The grievance or objection shall not result in the stay of execution of the
grieved-against or objected-to decision. The Committee shall decide on the
grievance or objection within (6) six working days as of the date of the
objection or grievance. 

Chapter
III

Admission
to Psychiatric Health Facility 

Article
16 - Types of Admission to the Psychiatric Health Facility 

The
Psychiatric Patient or the person shall be admitted to the Psychiatric Health
Facility for the purpose of Assessment; voluntary, compulsory or urgent
treatment or Placement. No Compulsory Admission to the private Psychiatric
Health Facilities may take place without the approval of the competent court or
Public Prosecution, in accordance with the controls and conditions defined by
such entities. 

Article
17- Voluntary Admission 

The
Voluntary Admission to the Psychiatric Health Facility for treatment shall be
made based on the written consent of the Psychiatric Patient or his
representative. Moreover, the Psychiatric Patient may leave such facility based
on his request or the request of his Representative, even if he didn’t
complete his treatment. 

Article
18 - Prohibition from Leaving the Facility 

The
Attending Physician may prohibit the Psychiatric Patient who was voluntarily
admitted to the Psychiatric Health Facility from leaving it in accordance with
the procedures and controls set forth under the Implementing Regulation of this
Law.

Article
19 - Compulsory Admission for Assessment 

The
Compulsory Admission of the person to the Psychiatric Health Facility for
Assessment shall be made by virtue of a decision by the Public Prosecution or a
ruling or decision by the competent Court. 

Article
20 - Procedures of the Compulsory Admission for Assessment

1-
The Attending Physician shall notify the person whose Compulsory Admission to
the Psychiatric Health Facility was decided, in the event his conditions allows
so, or his Representative of the reason for his admission upon initiation of the
procedures of the Compulsory Admission for Assessment. 

2-
The management of the Psychiatric Health Facility shall notify the Committee of
any person who is compulsorily admitted for Assessment within a period of not
more than (7) seven working days as of the date of the Admission decision.


3-
The duration of Compulsory Admission for Assessment shall not exceed (45) days,
renewable for the period deemed appropriate by the Committee, based on the
recommendation of the physician who performed the Assessment. 

4-
The Psychiatrist who performs the Assessment may decide to apply the Compulsory
Admission for Assessment or the Compulsory Outpatient Therapeutic Care, in such
a manner that does not violate or contradict the decision or the judicial ruling
on Assessment. 

Article
21 - Assessment Result Report 

The
management of the Psychiatric Health Facility shall prepare a report on the
Assessment result and shall refer the same to the Public Prosecution or
competent court as applicable. 

Article
22 - Compulsory Admission for Treatment 

The
Compulsory Admission of the Psychiatric Patient for Treatment shall be decided
by two Psychiatrists; one of them shall be in the same Psychiatric Health
Facility, provided that the management of the Psychiatric Health Facility is
notified of the same within (24) hours. 

Article
23 - Conditions of the Compulsory Admission for Treatment 

No
Psychiatric Patient may be compulsory admitted to the Psychiatric Health
Facility until fulfilment of the below conditions: 

1-
There should be clear indications that he suffers from an acute Psychiatric
Illness posing danger to him or Others. 

2-
His admission shall be necessary for his recovery or to stop the deterioration
of his health condition. 

Article
24 - Duration of the Compulsory Treatment 

The
duration of the Compulsory Treatment shall be defined based on the requirements
of the Psychiatric Patient’s health status, provided that it does not
exceed (45) days, renewable for the period deemed appropriate by the Committee
based on the recommendation of the Attending Physician. 

Article
25 - Notification of Cases of Compulsory Admission 

The
management of the Psychiatric Health Facility shall notify the Public
Prosecution of any case of Compulsory Admission for Treatment within (48) hours
and shall also notify the Committee within a period of not more than (7) seven
working days as of the date of the Admission decision. 

Article
26 - Reporting any Escape from the Health Facility 

In
case of escape of the Psychiatric Patient who is subject to Compulsory
Admission, the management of the Psychiatric Health Facility shall report the
same to the competent authorities and the Representative of the Psychiatric
Patient to return him to the Psychiatric Health Facility to continue the
procedures of Compulsory Treatment. 

Article
27 - Objection to the Compulsory Admission Decision 

The
Psychiatric Patient or his Representative may object before the Committee to the
decision on Compulsory Admission for Treatment or the extension of the duration
thereof. 

Article
28 - Termination of Compulsory Admission 

The
Compulsory Admission for Treatment shall be terminated by virtue of a decision
by the Attending Psychiatrist unless the Psychiatric Patient is placed by virtue
of a decision by the Public Prosecution or a ruling or decision by the competent
court.

Article
29 - Permit to Leave Temporarily 

The
Attending Physician may authorise the Psychiatric Patient to leave the
Psychiatric Health Facility temporarily, in accordance with the conditions,
controls and procedures provided for under the Implementing Regulation of this
Law, unless the Psychiatric Patient is placed by virtue of a decision by the
Public Prosecution or a ruling or decision by the competent court.

Article
30 - Transfer of the Psychiatric Patient to Treatment 

The
Psychiatric Patient who has been compulsorily admitted to a Psychiatric Health
Facility may be transferred to another health facility for treatment if he
becomes ill and no treatment is available for him at the Psychiatric Health
Facility where he is present, in accordance with the controls defined under a
decision by the Minister.

Article
31 - Receiving a Psychiatric Patient 

Unless
the Psychiatric Patient is capable of taking care of himself or is placed at the
Psychiatric Health Facility by virtue of a decision by the Public Prosecution or
a ruling or decision by the competent court, the Representative of the
Psychiatric Patient or the entity to which he was transferred shall undertake to
receive him at the end of his treatment in accordance with the procedures
applicable at the Psychiatric Health Facility. Should the representative or such
entity refuse to receive the Psychiatric Patient, the matter shall be referred
to the Public Prosecution to issue a decision requiring whomever it deems
appropriate to receive him. 

Article
32 - Death of the Psychiatric Patient 

1-
In case of death of the Psychiatric Patient, the Psychiatric Health Facility
shall notify his Representative, failing which it shall notify the Public
Prosecution. 

2-
In case of death of a Psychiatric Patient who is subject to the procedures of
Compulsory Admission for Assessment or Placement, the Psychiatric Health
Facility shall notify his Representative and inform the Public Prosecution of
the same. 

Article
33 - Emergency Admission 

If
a person is admitted to a Health Facility in emergency, showing signs of
Psychiatric Illness posing danger to himself or to Others, the Physician shall
hold him in custody and refer him to a Psychiatrist within a period of not more
than (24) hours from the time of holding him in custody for the purpose of
examination, diagnosis and provision of necessary health care. 

Article
34 - Holding a Person in Custody 

In
the event the Physician is unable to examine the person who is admitted to the
Health Facility in emergency and whose condition poses danger to himself or to
Others, the Psychiatric Nurse, Psychologist, Psychological Counsel, Social
Worker or Occupational Therapist shall hold him in custody at the Health
Facility for a period of not more than (8) hours and notify the Physician and
the management of Psychiatric Health Facility of the same. 

Article
35 - End of Custody Decision 

The
period of validity of the decision to hold a person in custody shall end with
the expiry of the periods provided for under Articles (33) and (34) of this Law
or in the presence of the Psychiatrist, in which case the Psychiatrist may
decide the Admission of the person to the Psychiatric Health Facility whenever
the conditions of the Compulsory Admission set forth under Article (23) hereof
apply to his condition or such person may be admitted voluntarily in accordance
with the provisions of Article (17) hereof. 

Article
36 - Seeking the Help of the Police or Rescue Team

It
is possible to seek the help of the Police or the Rescue Team or both to
transport the Psychiatric Patient or any person showing symptoms of a
Psychiatric Illness and who is difficult to control, poses danger to himself or
to Others and refused to be voluntarily admitted to the Psychiatric Health
Facility, in accordance with the controls and procedures defined under the
Implementing Regulation of this Law.

Article
37 - Entering the Private Places where the Psychiatric Patient is Present 

The
Police may - after obtaining an authorisation from the Public Prosecution -
enter the private places where a Psychiatric Patient suffering from a
Psychiatric Disorder is present and transport him to a Psychiatric Health
Facility in case the following is proven: 

1-
The Psychiatric Patient is not receiving the necessary treatment or is being
neglected or his behaviours are uncontrollable. 

2-
The Psychiatric Patient cannot rely on himself or cannot live alone due to his
Psychiatric Disorder. 

The
Implementing Regulation of this Law shall set forth the controls and procedures
for the implementation of the provisions of this Article.

Article
38 - Placement of the Accused Showing Symptoms of Psychiatric Illness 

Taking
into account the provisions of Federal Decree-Law No. (38) of 2022 mentioned
here-above, when considering the cases brought before them, the judicial
authorities may order the Placement of the accused who shows symptoms of a
Psychiatric Disorder and poses danger to himself or to Others for the purpose of
Assessment or Treatment. 

Article
39 - The Right to Assessment and Treatment 

The
persons whose freedom is restricted due to imprisonment, detention or remand may
not be deprived of the Assessment or the necessary Treatment in case they show
any symptoms of a Psychiatric Disorder. 

Chapter
IV

Compulsory
Outpatient Therapeutic Care

Article
40 - Controls of the Compulsory Outpatient Therapeutic Care

1-
The Psychiatric Patient shall be subject to the Compulsory Outpatient
Therapeutic Care as per a decision issued by the Psychiatrist based on a medical
recommendation or upon the request of the Psychiatric Patient’s
Representative to subject him to Compulsory Outpatient Therapeutic Care, in
which case the Representative shall undertake to abide by the prescribed
treatment plan. 

2-
The Psychiatrist shall issue his decision referred to under Clause (1) hereof
after verifying the following: 

a-The
person suffers of a Psychiatric Disorder. 

b-
The condition of the Psychiatric Patient requires the continuation of Treatment
without the need for being admitted to a Psychiatric Health Facility. 

c-The
condition of the Psychiatric Patient does not pose serious danger to his safety
or life or to the safety or life of Others. 

d-The
deterioration of the Psychiatric Patient’s condition in the event of
termination of his Treatment. 

3-
The management of the Psychiatric Health Facility shall follow up the condition
of the Patient and notify the Committee of the same within a period of not more
than (14) days from the date of implementation of the decision on Compulsory
Outpatient Therapeutic Care.

4-
Based on the recommendation of the Psychiatrist and the approval of the judicial
authority ordering the Placement, the Psychiatric Patient may be subjected to
Compulsory Outpatient Therapeutic Care in accordance with the procedures defined
under the Implementing Regulation of this Law.

5-
The duration of Compulsory Outpatient Therapeutic Care shall end with the end of
its purpose or necessity based on the report of the Psychiatrist. 

Article
41 - Impossibility of Compulsory Outpatient Therapeutic Care 

Taking
into account the provisions of Article (23) hereof, the Psychiatric Patient
shall be readmitted to the Psychiatric Health Facility for Treatment in case it
is impossible to subject him to Compulsory Outpatient Therapeutic Care or in
case he failed to comply with the prescribed treatment program. 

Article
42 – Request to Transfer Treatment 

The
Psychiatric Patient who is undergoing outpatient treatment or his Representative
may request the transfer of his Treatment to an external clinic at another
Psychiatric Health facility or to a private clinic in accordance with the
procedures defined by a decision by the Minister after coordination with the
Health Entity. 

Chapter
V

Controls
of the Psychiatric Patient Treatment 

Article
43 - Consent of the Psychiatric Patient to Compulsory Admission 

The
Psychiatrist may administer to the Psychiatric Patient who was compulsorily
admitted the necessary treatment whether with or without his consent, for a
renewable period of not more than (45) days from the date of the admission
decision, except in the following cases requiring the consent of the Psychiatric
Patient or his legal representative: 

1-
Electroconvulsive therapy except in emergency cases.

2-
Treatment of organic diseases of which the Psychiatric Patient suffers, except
in emergency cases.

3-The
special treatments determined by a decision of the Minister.

The
Implementing Regulation of this Law shall set out the controls for the
implementation of this Article.

Article
44 - Consent of the Psychiatric Patient to Voluntary Admission 

In
case of Voluntary Admission to the Psychiatric Health Facility and before the
provision of any treatment, the Psychiatrist shall obtain the consent of the
Psychiatric Patient or his Representative. 

Moreover,
the Psychiatrist shall write down the treatment plan and shall indicate the
consent or non-consent of the Psychiatric Patient or his Representative in his
medical file in addition to any material amendment made to the treatment plan or
its period in accordance with the controls and conditions defined under the
Implementing Regulation of this Law.

Article
45- Emergency Treatment 

Taking
into account the provisions of Article (43) of this Law, the Psychiatric Patient
who is compulsory admitted to the Health Facility may be subjected to Emergency
Treatment without his consent, including the Electroconvulsive therapy in
accordance with the recognised medical principles, based on the decision of the
Psychiatrist in both following cases: 

1-
If the condition of the Psychiatric Patient poses threat to his life or safety
or to the life or safety of Others. 

2-
If the treatment is imperative to prevent serious deterioration of the
Psychiatric Patient's condition. 

Article
46 - Withdrawal by the Psychiatric Patient of his Consent to Treatment 

Should
the Psychiatric Patient decide to withdraw his consent to the treatment he is
undergoing, such treatment shall be then discontinued except where the
provisions of Article (45) hereof apply to the condition. 

Article
47 - Psychiatric Patient Restraining or Isolation 

By
virtue of Article (48) here-below, the Psychiatric Patient may be only
restrained or isolated with the permission of the Psychiatrist, without the need
of obtaining the consent of the Psychiatric Patient. In emergency cases, the
nurse who is taking care of the Psychiatric Patient may have recourse to his
restraining or isolation, provided that he immediately informs the Psychiatrist
of the same, to examine him and define the duration of restraining or isolation
as he deems appropriate. In all cases, the Psychiatric Patient shall be
restrained or isolated only for the duration required by his health condition
and such restraining or isolation shall stop immediately if its reason no longer
exists.

The
Implementing Regulation of this Law shall set forth the procedures and controls
for the restraining and isolation of the Psychiatric Patient, in addition to the
controls related to the Isolation rooms. 

Article
48 - Cases of the Psychiatric Patient Restraining and Isolation 

The
Psychiatric Patient may be restrained and isolated only in the following cases:


1-
To prevent him from harming himself or Others. 

2-
To prevent him from destroying properties. 

Article
49 - Transport of the Psychiatric Patient

The
Psychiatric Patient may be transported inside the State or abroad only if his
health condition allows so, in accordance with a safe mechanism and based on a
medical report issued by the Psychiatric Health Facility proving the possibility
of transporting him. 

However,
in the event the Psychiatric Patient poses danger to himself or to Others, his
transportation to a Psychiatric Health Facility shall take place in accordance
with safe controls and requirements. The controls of the safe transportation of
the Psychiatric Patient shall be defined by a decision of the Minister. 

Article
50 - Applying Voluntarily for Addiction Treatment 

1-
It shall not be permissible to disclose any information or data to any entity
other than the Health Entities, the judicial authority and the security entities
about the persons who are admitted to a Health Facility for the purpose of
undergoing a treatment for drugs or psychotropic substances abuse, without
prejudice to the provisions of Federal Decree-Law No. (30) of 2021 on Fighting
Narcotics and Psychotropic Substances. 

2-
In the event the adult abuser or the guardian or tutor of a minor abuser
voluntarily applies to the Psychiatric Health Facility for the purpose of
undergoing a treatment for drugs or psychotropic substances abuse before a
warrant of arrest is issued against him, admission to the facility or leaving
such facility shall not require the approval of the Public Prosecution. In such
case no criminal lawsuit shall be filed against him. 

Chapter
VI

Penalties

Article
51

The
application of the penalties provided for in this Law shall be without prejudice
to any more severe penalty provided for in any other law.

Article
52

1-
Shall be punished by imprisonment and by a fine not less than AED (50,000) fifty
thousand and not exceeding AED (200,000) two hundred thousand , or by either of
these penalties :

1-
Whoever intentionally includes in his medical report any information contrary to
the reality regarding the Psychiatric condition of any person, with the
intention of admitting such person to or discharging him from the Psychiatric
Health Facility. 

2-
Whoever - acting in bad faith - causes a person to be admitted to a Psychiatric
Health Facility contrary to the provisions of this Law and its Implementing
Regulation. 

Article
53

Whoever
helps a person subject to Compulsory Admission procedures to escape the facility
shall be punished by imprisonment and by a fine of not less than AED (50,000)
fifty thousand and not exceeding AED (100,000) one hundred thousand, or by
either of these penalties. 

Article
54

Any
person who is in charge of guarding, caring for, nursing, or treating a person
suffering from a Psychiatric Illness and intentionally mistreats or neglects
him, shall be punished by imprisonment for a period no exceeding one year and a
fine of not less than AED (50,000) fifty thousand and not exceeding AED
(100,000) one hundred thousand , or by either of these penalties.

Should
such mistreatment or neglect result in a severe illness, serious injury or
physical disability of the Psychiatric Patient, the offender shall be then
punished by imprisonment for a period of not less than one year and a fine of
not less than AED (100,000) one hundred thousand and not exceeding AED (200,000)
two hundred thousand, or by either of these penalties.

Article
55

Any
of the penalties stipulated in this Decree-Law shall be doubled in case of
recidivism.

Article
56

The
penalties provided for under this Law shall not prevent any disciplinary
penalties from being imposed against the Psychiatric Health Facilities or any
party in case of violation of the provisions of this Law and its Implementing
Regulation, in accordance with the legislation applicable to this effect.

Chapter
VII

Final
Provisions

Article
57 - Law Enforcement Capacity

Law
enforcement capacity shall be granted to whomever is determined in a decision of
the Minister of Justice in agreement with the Minister or the Head of the Health
Entity, to detect the violations committed against the provisions of this Law,
its Implementing Regulation and its implementing decisions.

Article
58 - Care Homes

The
competent government entities, public welfare association, private sector and
individuals may establish care homes for the accommodation and care of
Psychiatric Patients whose condition does not requiring staying at the
Psychiatric Health Facility but have no family member to care for them or lack
necessary family care. The Implementing Regulation of this Law shall set forth
the conditions and controls of such homes. 

Article
59 - The Implementing Regulation

The
Council of Ministers shall, based on the Minister's proposal, issue the
Implementing Regulation for this Law within one year from the date of its
publication.

Article
60 - Abrogation

The
aforementioned Federal Law no. (28) of
1981 
shall be abrogated, as well as any provisions deemed contrary to or inconsistent
with the provisions of this Law .


Article
61 - Publication and Enforcement of the Law

This
Law shall be published in the Official Gazette, and shall come into force (6)
six months from the date of its publication.

Issued
by Us at the Presidential 
Palace in Abu Dhabi:-
On: 13/05/1445
H.
Corresponding to: 27/11/2023 


Mohammed bin
Zayed Al Nahyan

President of the
United Arab Emirates

(6) months from its publication (it was published in the Official Gazette, Issue
No. 764 (Annex) dated 30/11/2023). Accordingly, this Federal Decree-Law is
currently published as included in the Official Gazette without abrogating
Federal Law No. 28 dated 07/11/1981 concerning the Detention and Treatment of
People with a Mental Disorder until the expiry of the period mentioned
here-above, i.e. after six months from its publication, with respect to the text
of the Official Gazette and for information; thus, a mention is in order.

[2] 
Federal Law No. 28 dated 07/11/1981 concerning the
Detention and Treatment of People with a Mental Disorder mentioned here-above
will be abrogated only after the expiry of the period set under Article (61) of
this Federal Law, i.e. after (6) months from its publication (it was published
in the Official Gazette, Issue No. 764 (Annex) dated 30/11/2023).
